name: Double-Chocolate Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chocolate in the top of a double boiler; bring water to a boil.
2. Reduce heat to low and cook until chocolate melts completely.
3. Combine chocolate, flour, baking powder, soda, salt, sugar, butter, water, and vanilla in an electric mixer.
4. Beat for 2 minutes at medium speed.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating for an additional 2 minutes after each addition.
6. Pour batter into 4 greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans.
7. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 to 18 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Cool cake layers in pans for 10 minutes; remove from pans and let cool completely on wire racks.
9. Place one layer on a cake platter. Spread with half the Chocolate Filling.
10. Place another cake layer on top, and spread with half the Whipped Cream Frosting.
11. Repeat layers and garnish with grated chocolate if desired.
12. Refrigerate. Yield: one 4-layer torte.
